summary:
Prucalopride functions solely as a 5-HT4 receptor agonist, enhancing gut movement primarily via stimulation of serotonergic pathways without involving other types of receptors; all supporting studies align consistently around its exclusive classification within this category.
Prucalopride serves medicinally to treat chronic idiopathic constipation due to its specific action as a highly-selective 5-HT4 agonist promoting enhanced gastrointestinal activity while excluding antagonistic properties observed elsewhere among related compounds like tegaserod. Summary:  
Prucalopride works uniquely as a 5-HT4 receptor agonist, boosting intestinal function mainly through activating serotonin receptors; numerous research findings uniformly affirm this precise mode of operation free from contradictions regarding alternate mechanisms.

It treats chronic constipation effectively because of its targeted enhancement of digestive processes mediated strictly through interaction with 5-HT4 receptors alone.
